evidence_quotes.quote: Videogames were never made to only make profit, they were also created and built to tell a story, to hae fun together, to enjoy time with every person in the world.

paragraphs.text: Videogames were never made to only make profit, they were also created and built to tell a story, to hae fun together, to enjoy time with every person in the world. Nowadays games are being treated as a luxury, that only some are able to buy and play and paying for a license isn't the same, for people pay for their own money to escape reality to smile and hope for a better future. Games are meant to be fun, but we're losing that wish, that desire to stay together through playing our favourite games. Games are often talked bad because of some bad behaviour by those who play them, because of their questionable actions, but what about the multiple communities built for that certain videogame series, how many people were able to meet new friends though those? Through a new medium people have connected, laughed, cried, hoped and dreamt. Is it really evil to continue on wishing for simpler times and laughs? To continue connecting to even more people and share moments together? Why should we only focus on the negative parts and not look at the positives too? My wish is for games to be played by everyone, for both those that can't afford playing and those that can, to see communities growing even more and not to see lovable videogames ruined because of money and profit, because if those companies actually loved and cared the games that they created together, they would really understand our wish. Signed An European Citizen from an European Country
